title: WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: POLK COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves pregnant, breastfeeding people, and children up to 5 years old. The program provides nutritious supplemental foods, access to nutrition education and counseling, health screenings, and referrals to other services. 182 SW Academy Street Suite 302 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Applicants must meet the following criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is NOT required to participate in WIC) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding individual, or a child under the age of 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifying child. -Have a household income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: Varies by location Email (503) 623-8175 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 623-8175 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent/guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Polk County and surrounding areas SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Services: WIC Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CONNECTS SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINATIONS AND TUBERCULOSIS SCREENINGS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. POLK COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH
